_meta.description: NotebookLM notebook register for the Redline project. Single source of truth for notebook-agent access mapping. All other locations (agent JDs, agent-register.md) must reference this file — never restate ownership inline.
_meta.ssot: Governed by ADR-001. Notebook-agent access mapping is authoritative here; stating it elsewhere is a prohibited duplication.

schema.id: Unique kebab-case identifier for the notebook
schema.name: Display name shown in outputs and citations
schema.url: NotebookLM notebook URL (empty string if not yet created)
schema.description: What the notebook contains and its scope
schema.topic_area: One of: 'Engineering theory & standards' | 'Geotechnical domain' | 'AI & software architecture' | 'Process & product' | 'Founder strategy' | 'Communication & documentation'
schema.topics: Keyword array used for routing queries to the right notebook
schema.content_types: What kind of sources the notebook contains (books, official-docs, etc.)
schema.use_cases: Concrete query scenarios this notebook is suited to answer
schema.access: Permission gate: 'open' (all agents) | 'advisory-board-only' (Ron, Mark, John only)
schema.added: ISO 8601 date the notebook was registered
schema.owner: Agent id accountable for notebook freshness, scope decisions, and version pinning. One agent only.
schema.consumers: Array of agent ids with standing read access. They query the notebook directly without routing through the owner.

legend.owner: Accountable agent — maintains the notebook, controls what goes in, pins the framework/standards version. One owner per notebook. Changes to owner require Harriet sign-off.
legend.consumers: Standing read access — agents who query this notebook directly as part of their regular work. Consumers do not maintain the notebook. Changes to consumers require Harriet sign-off.
legend.access=open: All agents may query this notebook when routing logic selects it.
legend.access=advisory-board-only: Restricted to Ron, Mark, John. Other agents must request via Ron.
legend.empty-url: Notebook approved but not yet created in NotebookLM. Linda creates it on founder go.

notebooks.name: Engineering Standards
notebooks.description: NZ infrastructure design standards (IDS), earthworks and compaction requirements (NZS 4431), geotechnical QA procedures, and subdivision consent documentation for practitioners working in the Canterbury / NZ regulatory context.
use_cases: Checking NZ engineering design standards for geotechnical work
Verifying earthworks and compaction requirements
Understanding subdivision consent documentation requirements

notebooks.name: Ground Engineering Magazine (2014–2019)
notebooks.description: Issues of Ground Engineering magazine from 2014 to 2019 covering geotechnical industry news, technical papers, and key standards development (Eurocode 7, BS 5930, SPERWall, CIRIA) across piling, foundations, tunnelling, earthworks, and slope stability.
use_cases: Researching geotechnical engineering standards development (pre-2020)
Finding technical papers on piling, foundations, or slope stability
Understanding industry trends in geotechnical engineering 2014-2019

notebooks.name: Ground Engineering Magazine (2020–2026)
notebooks.description: Issues of Ground Engineering magazine from 2020 to 2026 covering geotechnical industry news, technical papers, and current standards development (Eurocode 7 revision, BS 5930) across piling, foundations, tunnelling, earthworks, and slope stability.
use_cases: Researching current geotechnical engineering standards development (2020-present)
Finding recent technical papers on piling, foundations, or slope stability
Understanding current industry trends in geotechnical engineering

notebooks.name: Geotechnical Baseline Reports (GBR)
notebooks.description: Industry guidance on preparing and contractually applying GBRs to allocate subsurface construction risk. Covers Differing Site Conditions claims, ASCE guidelines, CIRIA C807, the NZTS guide, and the FIDIC Emerald Book.
use_cases: Preparing geotechnical baseline reports
Understanding subsurface risk allocation in contracts
Researching differing site conditions claims

notebooks.name: Geotechnical Engineering Report Workflows and Standard Procedures
notebooks.description: Professional practice guides and authoritative references for geotechnical report structuring, factual/interpretive delineation, section-by-section authoring, and client-centric writing. Current sources (4): EnggNZ/NZGS Geotechnical Reports Guideline and Template (August 2023); CEDD GeoGuide 2 — Guide to Site Investigation (2017); CEDD GeoGuide 3 — Guide to Rock and Soil Descriptions (2017); FHWA-NHI-16-072 Geotechnical Engineering Circular No. 5 (2016). Populated 2026-06-09 per Graeme binding domain verdicts.
use_cases: Structuring geotechnical engineering reports
Applying factual/interpretive delineation in reports
Using authoritative guidance for section-by-section report authoring
Applying NZ professional practice standards for geotechnical reports
Referencing FHWA or CEDD GeoGuide standards for site investigation and soil/rock description

notebooks.name: Risk Assessment in Engineering
notebooks.description: Risk management, professional liability, and probabilistic risk assessment in geotechnical engineering. Sources span ASCE, CIRIA, and NZGS guidelines, field manuals, and legal notes covering contracts, non-disclosure agreements, and professional indemnity insurance.
use_cases: Assessing geotechnical engineering risk
Understanding professional liability in engineering
Reviewing contract and insurance requirements

notebooks.name: Geotechnical Engineering Checklists
notebooks.description: A curated collection of geotechnical engineering QC checklists, review guidelines, and submittal checklists from US and NZ sources. Covers geotechnical report review (FHWA), geotechnical design QC workflow, earthquake geotechnical investigation practice (NZGS Module 2), ground investigation specification, and building consent lodgement requirements.
use_cases: Reviewing a geotechnical report for completeness and quality
Checking required content for a soils and geology report or foundation report
Understanding FHWA or USACE requirements for geotechnical documentation
Verifying a geotechnical report meets NZ building consent lodgement requirements
Applying NZGS ground investigation specifications to site investigation planning

notebooks.name: AI System Engineering
notebooks.description: Generative AI application design and deployment: foundation models, prompt engineering, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), multiagent orchestration, and FastAPI/Docker serving. Covers MCP, OAuth2, the NIST AI Risk Management Framework, the EU AI Act, and GDPR.
use_cases: Designing generative AI applications
Implementing RAG or multiagent systems
Understanding AI governance and compliance requirements

notebooks.name: Software Architecture & Domain-Driven Design
notebooks.description: Ten canonical books (Evans, Vernon, Khononov, Ford, Ousterhout, GoF, and others) covering DDD strategic/tactical patterns (Bounded Contexts, Ubiquitous Language, Aggregates, Repositories, Domain Events), architecture styles (Hexagonal, CQRS, Microservices, Event-Driven), SOLID principles, GoF design patterns, functional domain modeling, EventStorming, Context Mapping, Event Sourcing, saga patterns, and Data Mesh.
use_cases: Applying DDD patterns to Redline architecture
Choosing architecture styles for new modules
Reviewing design patterns for domain modeling

notebooks.name: Software Development Methodology & Engineering Organisation
notebooks.description: 25-source knowledge base spanning software engineering methodology, product discovery, AI/LLM engineering, and engineering leadership. Engineering methodology: Farley's Modern Software Engineering, Mythical Man-Month, Software Engineering at Google, Accelerate (Forsgren et al.), Continuous Delivery, Extreme Programming, Clean Agile, Sprint. Product management and discovery: INSPIRED and Empowered (Cagan), Continuous Discovery Habits (Torres), Lean Product Playbook, Product-Led Growth, Project to Product. AI and LLM engineering: LLM Engineers Handbook (Iusztin), Age of Invisible Machines (Wilson), Co-Intelligence (Mollick), Designing Multi-Agent Systems (Dibia), AI Engineering Building Applications, Building Applications with AI Agents, Building Generative AI Services with FastAPI, Prompt Engineering for LLMs. Engineering organisation and leadership: Staff Engineer (Larson), Team Topologies, Working Backwards (Amazon — Bryar and Carr).
use_cases: Researching software engineering best practices and methodology
Designing or improving product discovery and continuous delivery workflows
Understanding LLM engineering, prompt engineering, and AI agent system design
Applying empowered product team frameworks from Cagan and Torres
Navigating engineering career development to staff-level and beyond
Grounding Amazon Working Backwards methodology in product development
Aligning engineering team structure and flow using Team Topologies

notebooks.name: Business Process Management
notebooks.description: BPM methodologies and continuous process improvement tools including Lean, Six Sigma, BPMN, 7FE, PDCA, and DMAIC frameworks. Drawn from expert-authored management books and the BPM Common Body of Knowledge (BPM CBOK).
use_cases: Designing or improving business processes
Applying Lean or Six Sigma to engineering workflows
Modelling processes with BPMN

notebooks.name: Product Roadmapping
notebooks.description: Strategic product roadmapping for product leaders: defining vision, prioritising customer opportunities, and achieving stakeholder buy-in. Covers Kano model, MoSCoW prioritisation, ROI scorecards, user journey maps, and opportunity-solution trees.
use_cases: Building or refreshing the product roadmap
Prioritising features using Kano or MoSCoW
Creating opportunity-solution trees

notebooks.name: Writing Painless Product and Functional Specifications
notebooks.description: Software product management frameworks and specification writing: PRDs, functional specifications, OKRs, the Shape Up framework, product discovery vs delivery, and alternatives to traditional roadmaps.
use_cases: Writing product requirement documents
Structuring functional specifications
Choosing between product discovery and delivery approaches

notebooks.name: Organisational Design & Team Topologies
notebooks.description: A curated collection of books and frameworks on modern organisational leadership, team design, and workforce development. Covers Team Topologies (stream-aligned, platform, enabling teams), Conway's Law, cognitive load management, AI/RPA task deconstruction, deliberate practice, skills-based organisations, and Domain-Driven Design (DDD) for software teams. Case studies from Google, GE, and large healthcare providers. Also contains The Pillars (ACE New Zealand, 2024) — a non-technical competency framework for the NZ engineering and consulting sector, covering four pillars: Relationship Management, Talent Management, Operational Excellence, and Professionalism and Culture.
topics: AI and automation in the workplace
use_cases: Restructuring software engineering teams around stream-aligned or platform topology
Deciding which tasks to automate with AI or RPA versus retain for human workers
Designing deliberate practice programs to develop world-class technical talent
Transitioning from role-based to skills-based hiring and performance management
Applying Conway's Law and DDD to align team structure with software architecture
Assessing non-technical competency gaps in NZ engineering and consulting staff using the ACE Pillars framework

notebooks.name: Professional Services Firm Management
notebooks.description: Comprehensive guide to the business, management, and future of professional service firms (PSFs) — engineering, law, and consulting practices. Covers individual career development (resume writing, networking, PE licensure), firm creation and entrepreneurship, day-to-day operations (project management, client relations, IDC), financial and human capital management, A/E/C firm strategy, and technological disruption (the Innovator's Dilemma, AI impact on professions). Contains practical templates, checklists, and worksheets alongside theoretical frameworks from published books and management literature.
use_cases: Managing or growing a professional services or engineering firm
Planning a career in engineering or consulting
Assessing risks from technological disruption to professional service business models
Improving firm operations — project management, client relations, and profitability
Researching A/E/C firm strategy, niche positioning, and market differentiation

notebooks.name: Government Contracting, Proposal Management & Systems Engineering
notebooks.description: Comprehensive guide to winning government contracts (GovCon). Covers requirements engineering (RE), Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) using UML and SysML, proposal strategy and management, RFP compliance and compliance matrices, and AI-assisted proposal automation including Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) for hallucination-free draft generation. Contains academic textbooks, practical playbooks, compliance matrix templates, decision matrices, and checklists.
use_cases: Preparing a winning bid for a government contract
Building requirements engineering frameworks for complex system design
Adopting AI to automate proposal compliance and draft generation
Diagnosing and fixing broken proposal processes
Translating vague customer requirements into precise, testable system specifications

notebooks.name: Strategy & Competitive Advantage
notebooks.description: Comprehensive guide to strategic thinking, competitive positioning, and sustained advantage. Covers business strategy frameworks (Good Strategy Bad Strategy, Blue Ocean), competitive analysis, resource-based view of the firm, strategic inflection points, and how organizations build defensible competitive moats. Draws on canonical strategy books and case studies for product, firm, and market-level strategic thinking.
use_cases: Developing a coherent strategy with clear diagnosis, guiding policy, and coherent actions
Analysing competitive positioning and identifying sustainable competitive advantages
Stress-testing strategic assumptions and identifying strategic risks
Evaluating Blue Ocean vs Red Ocean opportunities
Understanding how firms build and defend competitive moats

notebooks.name: Monetizing & Scaling Innovation
notebooks.description: Strategic guide on building profitable, scalable products. Contains Monetizing Innovation and Scaling Innovation by Madhavan Ramanujam et al. Covers willingness-to-pay discovery, pricing models (subscription, usage-based, outcome-based), packaging and bundling (Good-Better-Best tiers), behavioural pricing tactics (anchoring, compromise effect), the four monetisation failure patterns (Feature Shock, Minivation, Hidden Gem, Undead), and scaling strategies covering churn prevention, wallet-share growth, and price negotiation discipline.
use_cases: Deciding how to price a new product, feature tier, or service
Evaluating packaging and Good-Better-Best tier strategies
Preparing for a company-wide price increase or sales negotiation
Designing a product go-to-market strategy grounded in customer willingness-to-pay
Researching known monetisation failure patterns before a product launch

notebooks.name: Entrepreneurship & Startup Strategy
notebooks.description: Comprehensive guide for entrepreneurial management, strategic growth, and corporate innovation. Covers disruptive innovation, Lean Startup methodology (MVP, Build-Measure-Learn), customer development and Jobs-to-Be-Done theory, product-market fit, Crossing the Chasm (early adopters vs mainstream), B2B sales tactics, product roadmapping, and the Business Model Canvas. Drawn from authoritative business books, case studies, and operational frameworks.
use_cases: Launching a new venture and identifying a scalable business model
Bringing a disruptive technology to market without falling into traditional management traps
Restructuring a mature organisation to foster an entrepreneurial culture
Designing a B2B sales strategy targeting the right corporate decision-makers
Creating a product roadmap that aligns teams around customer value

notebooks.name: Product Design & UX
notebooks.description: Four canonical texts on human-centred design and usability: The Design of Everyday Things (Norman), Don't Make Me Think Revisited (Krug), Designing with Data, and Forms that Work. Covers affordances, signifiers, the psychology of human error, A/B testing, usability testing, web navigation, and form design.
use_cases: Designing intuitive web interfaces or digital products using HCD principles
Setting up usability tests or A/B experiments to resolve design debates
Optimising form design for user comprehension and completion rates
Diagnosing why users make errors or abandon a system
Grounding product UX decisions in psychology and empirical evidence

notebooks.name: DevOps & GCP Infrastructure
notebooks.description: Eight books equipping Brent (DevOps/GCP agent) with source material for GCP operations, CI/CD, infrastructure as code, and SOC 2 controls. Covers cloud-native security (Armitage), GCP in depth (Blum, Kanjani), DevSecOps practices and tooling (Suehring), serverless security across AWS/Azure/GCP (Lat), Terraform IaC on GCP (Wang), SOC 2 compliance (SecuriCheck), and the foundational DevOps Handbook (Kim et al.).
use_cases: Understanding GCP services and deployment patterns for production workloads
Implementing DevSecOps practices and shift-left security in CI/CD pipelines
Provisioning and managing GCP infrastructure with Terraform
Assessing and remediating serverless security risks on AWS, Azure, and GCP
Working towards or auditing SOC 2 compliance for cloud-hosted SaaS products

notebooks.name: Engineers' Guide to Technical Communication
notebooks.description: Technical writing and editing best practices for engineering and scientific audiences: document design, audience analysis, ethics, citation standards (Chicago Manual of Style), and structure for reports, proposals, laboratory studies, specifications, and business correspondence.
use_cases: Structuring engineering reports and proposals
Applying citation and documentation standards
Improving technical writing quality

notebooks.name: GCP DevOps Tactical Playbook: Filling the 6 Library Gaps
notebooks.description: Official Google Cloud documentation covering GCP operational patterns, deployment guides, IAM configuration, Cloud Run, API references, and DevSecOps controls. Complements the foundational DevOps & GCP Infrastructure notebook (8 books) with authoritative first-party Google source material targeting 6 coverage gaps identified during the Brent topology screen (2026-06-06).
use_cases: Looking up authoritative GCP API references, CLI patterns, and service configurations during infrastructure provisioning
Verifying current GCP deployment patterns for Cloud Run, IAM least-privilege, and Cloud SQL
Grounding Brent's 12 DevOps skill files with official GCP documentation
Cross-referencing GCP security controls and SOC 2 mappings against official Google documentation

notebooks.name: Web API Design: REST Endpoints, Authentication and File Responses
notebooks.description: Framework-agnostic API design knowledge optimised to answer five design questions for issue #51: (1) where auth boundary sits, (2) sync vs async response for file generation, (3) correct HTTP response shape for downloadable DOCX, (4) monolith vs microservice boundary decision, (5) API-to-domain layer dependency structure. Sources: Building Microservices 2e (Newman), Microservices Up and Running (Mitra & Nadareishvili), Strategic Monoliths and Microservices (Vernon), Mastering API Architecture (Gough, Bryant, Auburn).
use_cases: Deciding where the authentication boundary sits in a REST API
Choosing between sync and async response patterns for file generation endpoints
Designing the correct HTTP response shape for a downloadable DOCX file
Making a monolith vs microservice boundary decision for a new service
Understanding API-to-domain layer dependency structure

notebooks.name: FastAPI Implementation: Building Python Web APIs
notebooks.description: FastAPI implementation knowledge — endpoint design, dependency injection, auth middleware, request/response models, file responses, and AI service integration with FastAPI. Sources: AI and Microservices (AI into API Design), Building Python Microservices with FastAPI, FastAPI Cookbook (Ferraro), FastAPI Modern Python Web Development, Building Generative AI Services with FastAPI (Foster/Parandeh).
use_cases: Implementing FastAPI endpoints with Pydantic request/response models
Configuring auth middleware and dependency injection in FastAPI
Returning downloadable file responses (StreamingResponse, FileResponse) from FastAPI
Integrating AI/LLM services into a FastAPI application
Async endpoint patterns and ASGI concurrency in FastAPI

notebooks.name: Django Application Development
notebooks.description: Django framework knowledge: fundamentals, ORM, DRF/REST APIs, TDD/testing, async/Channels, and production deployment configuration (Docker, PostgreSQL, collectstatic, ASGI/WSGI). Source of truth for Django implementation decisions.
use_cases: Framework API recall
ORM query patterns
DRF serializer and viewset design
Testing setup with pytest-django
Deployment configuration
Capability and limitation checks for design

notebooks.name: Office Open XML (OOXML) File Formats
notebooks.description: The Office Open XML (OOXML) / ECMA-376 file format and its programmatic manipulation in Python. Covers the ZIP-package structure, parts, relationships, content types, and the WordprocessingML hierarchy (paragraphs, runs, text), plus python-docx for creating and modifying .docx files. Sources (6): ECMA-376 standard landing page; python-docx docs and source repo; the van Vugt 'Open XML: The Markup Explained' PDF (live brandwares.com copy — original openlibra host dead); Microsoft's WordprocessingML structure doc; and the officeopenxml.com anatomy reference added via a stable Wayback snapshot (host currently unreachable).
use_cases: Understanding the OOXML / ECMA-376 package and part structure
Generating and modifying .docx files with python-docx
Mapping the WordprocessingML element hierarchy (paragraphs, runs, text)
Looking up content-types and relationship conventions for a Word document
Grounding the geotechnical compliance tool's Word document-generation backend

notebooks.name: MS Word Taskpane Add-in Development
notebooks.description: Modern web-based Microsoft Office Add-in development for Word task panes, built with the Office JavaScript API (Office.js), HTML/CSS/JS, the unified manifest, Fluent UI, and a Python backend pattern. Sources (9): Office Add-ins platform overview, Word task-pane tutorial, and UX design patterns (Microsoft Learn); Fluent 2 Design System; OfficeDev repos generator-office, Office-Add-in-samples, and office-js-snippets (Script Lab); the Masterjx9 Python/Flask TaskPane template; and CaseStrainer as a Python-backed add-in blueprint. EXCLUDED per founder flag: 'Building Office Add-ins using Office.js' (Zlatkovsky, paid + predates the unified manifest).
use_cases: Building a Word task-pane add-in with the Office JavaScript API
Scaffolding an Office add-in project with generator-office
Wiring a Python/Flask backend to an Office task pane
Applying Fluent UI and Office UX design patterns to a task pane
Looking up Office.js snippets and Microsoft 365 code samples
